title: The Battle of the Department of Mysteries
prose: The story reaches the school in pieces, and every piece is worse than the one before: Harry Potter and half a dozen others — DA members, some of them people you know by name — went to the Ministry of Magic itself in the dead of night, into a battle against Death Eaters in a room full of prophecy orbs. Sirius Black, cleared at last of the crime that sent him to Azkaban, was killed there, struck down by his own cousin.

It's the confirmation nobody in power could keep denying any longer: Voldemort is unmistakably, publicly back, and the Ministry has to admit it at last, live in the Daily Prophet, a full year after most of the school had already worked it out for themselves.

Dolores Umbridge doesn't return to Hogwarts after that night — the details of exactly what happened to her stay murky and much-speculated-about, but her post as High Inquisitor simply evaporates, decrees and all, and the noticeboard is bare again by the time term ends.

choices.label: Relief that it's finally acknowledged, grief for what it cost.
Mostly, you're afraid of what comes next.
